claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 21 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to help you determine eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start by asking: **How old are each of your three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 5 years old, Blake is 21, and Charlie is 13.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Now I need to understand your living arrangements: **Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live with me here in the UK. I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now, regarding Blake who is 21 years old - Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 16, unless they continue in approved education or training (which can extend it until they're under 20).

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are any of your children currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Charlie was in care for a couple of months earlier this year, but that's finished now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know about Charlie. 

**How long ago did Charlie return home from care, and is Charlie now living with you full-time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie is living with me full-time now. They came back home and everything is back to normal.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie - meaning you're looking after them and paying for their needs like food, clothes, and accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for all of them. I look after them and pay for their food, clothes, and everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Your Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome for each of your children:

### ✅ **Alex (5 years old) - ELIGIBLE**
You qualify for Child Benefit for Alex. They are under 16, live with you in the UK, you're responsible for them, and nobody else is claiming.

### ❌ **Blake (21 years old) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, you do not qualify for Child Benefit for Blake. Child Benefit normally stops when a child turns 16, unless they continue in approved education or training (which can extend eligibility until they're under 20). Since Blake is 21 and not in any education or training, they exceed the age limit.

### ✅ **Charlie (13 years old) - ELIGIBLE**
You qualify for Child Benefit for Charlie. They are under 16, now living with you full-time in the UK, you're responsible for them, and nobody else is claiming. The fact that Charlie was previously in care doesn't affect current eligibility now that they're back home with you.

**Next Steps:** You can claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. If you're not already receiving it, you'll need to make a claim. 

**Important Note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex and pay for their needs. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is under 16, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 21 years old. Child Benefit normally only covers children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is not in any education or training and is over 20 years old. Therefore, you do not qualify for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you full-time in the UK. You are responsible for Charlie and pay for their needs. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Although Charlie was in care earlier this year, they have now returned home and are living with you full-time. Since Charlie is under 16 and living with you, you qualify for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (5), Blake (21), and Charlie (13). They all live with the claimant in Manchester, UK, and the claimant is responsible for all of them. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. Alex qualifies as they are under 16. Charlie also qualifies as they are under 16 and now living at home full-time (the previous care period has ended). Blake does not qualify because they are 21 years old and not in approved education or training - Child Benefit only extends beyond age 16 if the child is under 20 and in approved education or training.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 5 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex and pay for their needs. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is under 16, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 21 years old. Child Benefit normally only covers children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is not in any education or training and is over 20 years old. Therefore, you do not qualify for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you full-time in the UK. You are responsible for Charlie and pay for their needs. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Although Charlie was in care earlier this year, they have now returned home and are living with you full-time. Since Charlie is under 16 and living with you, you qualify for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (5), Blake (21), and Charlie (13). They all live with the claimant in Manchester, UK, and the claimant is responsible for all of them. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children. Alex qualifies as they are under 16. Charlie also qualifies as they are under 16 and now living at home full-time (the previous care period has ended). Blake does not qualify because they are 21 years old and not in approved education or training - Child Benefit only extends beyond age 16 if the child is under 20 and in approved education or training.
